name: in-page-navigation
title: In-page navigation
description: An "On this page" jump list: a nav landmark of anchor links to the section headings of a long content page. Uncontrolled by default, it tracks the section in view with an IntersectionObserver (SSR-safe: attached on mount, torn down on unmount) and marks the active link with aria-current="location" plus non-color cues (an inline-start accent bar and heavier weight) so it survives forced-colors mode. Nested h2/h3 levels indent with logical properties that mirror in RTL; clicking smooth-scrolls (respecting prefers-reduced-motion), moves focus to the target section, and updates the hash normally. Optionally collapses behind a disclosure on narrow viewports. Pass an explicit items array, or derive them from the DOM with the opt-in useHeadings hook.
useWhen: A long content or policy page with several headed sections readers scan and jump between
Providing an "On this page" / table-of-contents sidebar next to article body copy
Letting readers see which section they are currently reading via scrollspy
avoidWhen: Navigating between different pages of a site (use a nav menu or breadcrumbs)
Paging through a result set (use Pagination)
A short page with one or two sections that needs no in-page index
Stepping through a linear multi-step task (use a step indicator)

keyboard: Tab moves through the anchor links in order; each is a native link and a regular tab stop
Enter activates a link: it smooth-scrolls to the section (or jumps under reduced motion), updates the hash normally, and moves focus to the target section
When collapsed on narrow viewports, Tab reaches the disclosure toggle first; Enter or Space expands the list (Base UI wires aria-expanded / aria-controls)
Browser Back is never hijacked — the hash is updated with a normal history entry
accessibility.targetSize: 44px project default (each link row is min-h-11)
notes: The list is a <nav aria-label> landmark wrapping an ordered list; the active entry carries aria-current="location" (a location within the current page), never aria-current="page".

Non-color redundancy: the active entry is bolded and gains an inline-start accent bar in addition to aria-current, so the current section stays identifiable when the accent color is overridden in Windows High Contrast / forced-colors mode. Every row keeps a transparent inline-start border so the box is identical across states.

Scrollspy uses an IntersectionObserver created on mount and disconnected on unmount; on the server (or where IntersectionObserver is unavailable) no observer is attached and no entry is marked active.
Nested h2/h3 indentation and the accent bar use logical properties (ps-*, border-s-*) so they mirror automatically in RTL.
Clicking moves focus to the target section — making it programmatically focusable with tabindex=-1 if it is not already — so keyboard and screen-reader users land in the content, not back at the top.
Links are underlined on hover and focus; the active state never depends on color alone.
